labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Recommended for hypertension in diabetes with CKD when albuminuria is not present
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: Angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor (ACEi) or angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB) should be first-line therapy for hypertension (HTN) when albuminuria is present, otherwise dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker (CCB) or diuretic can also be considered
